content:
First, observe that if we break the string of \(n\) ones into blocks of lengths \(1,2,3\) only, say \(m_1\) blocks of “1”, \(m_2\) blocks of “11”, and \(m_3\) blocks of “111”, then the total is

\[
m_1\cdot1 \;+\; m_2\cdot11 \;+\; m_3\cdot111 \;=\;1000.
\]

Write this as
\[
m_1 +11m_2+111m_3=1000.
\]
Then the total number of digits used is
\[
n \;=\; m_1\cdot1 + m_2\cdot2 + m_3\cdot3
\;=\;\bigl(1000-11m_2-111m_3\bigr)+2m_2+3m_3
\;=\;1000-9\,(m_2+12m_3).
\]
Set \(t=m_2+12m_3\).  The non‐negative integer solutions \((m_2,m_3)\) make \(t\) run over all integers from \(0\) up to \(106\) except \(107\), and also \(t=108\).  Hence there are
\[
\underbrace{107}_{t=0,1,2,\dots,106}\;+\;1\;=\;108
\]
distinct values of \(t\), and thus 108 distinct values of \(n=1000-9t\).  Therefore
\[
a=108,
\]
and the sum of the digits of \(a\) is
\[
1+0+8=9.
\]
\boxed{9}

message.content:

\[
m_1\cdot1 \;+\; m_2\cdot11 \;+\; m_3\cdot111 \;=\;1000.
\]

Write this as
\[
m_1 +11m_2+111m_3=1000.
\]
Then the total number of digits used is
\[
n \;=\; m_1\cdot1 + m_2\cdot2 + m_3\cdot3
\;=\;\bigl(1000-11m_2-111m_3\bigr)+2m_2+3m_3
\;=\;1000-9\,(m_2+12m_3).
\]
Set \(t=m_2+12m_3\).  The non‐negative integer solutions \((m_2,m_3)\) make \(t\) run over all integers from \(0\) up to \(106\) except \(107\), and also \(t=108\).  Hence there are
\[
\underbrace{107}_{t=0,1,2,\dots,106}\;+\;1\;=\;108
\]

\[
a=108,

\[

\]
\boxed{9}
